name: Mulligatawny Soup
recipeIngredient: 16 oz Can tomatoes, cut up, undrained
1 medium Cooking apple, peeled and chopped
Bony chicken pieces (backs, necks, and wings) from 2 chickens
3 stalks celery with leaves, cut up
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the broth, chicken, undrained tomatoes, apple, onion, carrot, celery, green pepper, parsley, lemon juice, sugar, curry, ground cloves, salt, and pepper.
2. Bring to boiling; reduce heat.
3. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
CHICKEN STOCK:
1. In a large stockpot or Dutch oven, place chicken pieces, celery, carrot, onion, salt, pepper, and cloves.
2. Add the water. Bring to boiling.
3. Reduce heat; cover and simmer for 1 hour.
4. Remove chicken.
5. Strain stock. Discard vegetables.
CROCK POT DIRECTIONS:
Use ingredients as above, except use only 4 cups water. Combine all ingredients in an electric slow cooker. Cover; cook on low-heat setting for 8 to 10 hours. Strain stock. Discard vegetables. Continue with the remaining instructions from the recipe.
[My notes: I usually don't have a bunch of 'bony' chicken pieces laying around the house. When I make my chicken stock, I just throw the whole darn chicken in the pot, cover it with water and boil for 1 hour. If I have the veggies to put in, wonderful; if not, tuff luck. Even without the veggies in the stock, the Mulligatawny is very good. Not too hot or spicy, but very flavorful. The cut-up vegetables really fill up the soup bowl and your tummy, as well! Well worth the effort to make. -sr]
[Better Homes & Gardens New Cook Book]
